title: Reversible Merge Sort on Doubly Linked List

statement.markdown: Given a doubly linked list, implement a reversible merge sort that can sort the list in ascending order and then reverse it back to its original order. Assume that the linked list nodes are numbered from 0 to n-1.
statement.inputFormat: The input will be a doubly linked list represented as a list of integers, where each integer is the value of a node. The length of the input list will be between 1 and 10000.
statement.outputFormat: The function should return a doubly linked list in the same order as the input list.
statement.notes: You need to preserve the original order of the list after sorting it. The time complexity of your solution should be O(n log n) and space complexity should be O(1), where n is the length of the input list.

examples.explanation: This is an example of a doubly linked list that is sorted in ascending order. The function should reverse the list after sorting it.

hints.text: Start by implementing a normal merge sort algorithm, which will sort the list in ascending order.
After sorting the list, implement a reverse function that will reverse the sorted list.
To reverse the list, you can use a two-pointer approach, where one pointer points to the head of the list and the other pointer points to the tail of the list.

editorial.title: Reversible Merge Sort on Doubly Linked List Editorial
editorial.intuition: The correct approach is to implement a merge sort algorithm, which divides the list into smaller sublists, sorts each sublist, and then merges the sorted sublists into a single sorted list.

buggyStarterCode.cpp: class Node { public: int data; Node* next; Node* prev; }; class DoublyLinkedList { public: Node* head; DoublyLinkedList() { head = NULL; } void reverse() { Node* current = head; while (current) { current->prev = current->next; current = current->next; } } void mergeSort(list Node*> &alist){ if (alist.empty()) return; list Node*> right = alist, mid = alist, left = alist; while (right->next) right = right->next, mid = mid->next, left = left->next; merge(left, mid); merge(mid, right); }
buggyStarterCode.java: class Node { public int data; Node next; Node prev; }; class DoublyLinkedList { public Node head; DoublyLinkedList() { head = null; } void reverse() { Node current = head; while (current) { current.prev = current.next; current = current.next; } } void mergeSort(ArrayList<Node> alist){ if (alist.isEmpty()) return; ArrayList<Node> right = alist, mid = alist, left = alist; while (right.get(right.size() - 1) != null) right = right.subList(1, right.size()); while (mid.get(mid.size() - 1) != null) mid = mid.subList(1, mid.size()); while (left.get(left.size() - 1) != null) left = left.subList(1, left.size()); merge(left, mid); merge(mid, right); }

solutions.python:
class Node:
    def __init__(self, data=None):
        self.data = data
        self.next = None
        self.prev = None

class DoublyLinkedList:
    def __init__(self):
        self.head = None

    def mergeSort(self):
        if not self.head:
            return

        right = self.head
        mid = self.head
        left = self.head

        while right.next:
            right = right.next
            mid = mid.next
            left = left.next

        self.mergeSortHelper(left, mid, right)

        current = self.head

        while current:
            current.prev = current.next
            current = current.next

    def mergeSortHelper(self, left, mid, right):
        while left != mid:
            temp = left.data
            left.data = right.data
            right.data = temp

            left = left.next
            right = right.prev

        self.mergeSortHelper(mid, right, right.next)

    def merge(self, left, mid, right):
        while left != mid:
            temp = left.data
            left.data = mid.data
            mid.data = temp

            left = left.next
            right = right.prev

        while mid != right:
            temp = mid.data
            mid.data = right.data
            right.data = temp

            mid = mid.next
            right = right.prev



class Solution:
    def solve(self):
        dll = DoublyLinkedList()

        dll.head = Node(5)
        dll.head.next = Node(2)
        dll.head.next.prev = dll.head
        dll.head.next.next = Node(8)
        dll.head.next.next.prev = dll.head.next
        dll.head.next.next.next = Node(3)
        dll.head.next.next.next.prev = dll.head.next.next
        dll.head.next.next.next.next = Node(1)
        dll.head.next.next.next.next.prev = dll.head.next.next.next
        dll.head.next.next.next.next.next = Node(4)
        dll.head.next.next.next.next.next.prev = dll.head.next.next.next.next
        dll.head.next.next.next.next.next.next = Node(6)
        dll.head.next.next.next.next.next.next.prev = dll.head.next.next.next.next.next

        dll.mergeSort()

        return dll.head


solution = Solution()

result = solution.solve()

print(result.data, end=' ')
for i in range(6):
    print(result.next.data, end=' ')
    result = result.next
solutions.cpp: class Node { public: int data; Node* next; Node* prev; }; class DoublyLinkedList { public: Node* head; DoublyLinkedList() { head = NULL; } void mergeSortHelper(Node* left, Node* mid, Node* right){ while (left != mid){ int temp = left->data; left->data = right->data; right->data = temp; left = left->next; right = right->prev; } mergeSortHelper(mid, right, right->next); } void mergeSort(){ if (!head) return; Node* right = head; Node* mid = head; Node* left = head; while (right->next) right = right->next, mid = mid->next, left = left->next; mergeSortHelper(left, mid, right); Node* current = head; while (current){ current->prev = current->next; current = current->next; } } void merge(Node* left, Node* mid, Node* right){ while (left != mid){ int temp = left->data; left->data = mid->data; mid->data = temp; left = left->next; right = right->prev; } while (mid != right){ int temp = mid->data; mid->data = right->data; right->data = temp; mid = mid->next; right = right->prev; } } }; class Solution { public: Node* solve(){ DoublyLinkedList dll; dll.head = new Node(5); dll.head->next = new Node(2); dll.head->next->prev = dll.head; dll.head->next->next = new Node(8); dll.head->next->next->prev = dll.head->next; dll.head->next->next->next = new Node(3); dll.head->next->next->next->prev = dll.head->next->next; dll.head->next->next->next->next = new Node(1); dll.head->next->next->next->next->prev = dll.head->next->next->next; dll.head->next->next->next->next->next = new Node(4); dll.head->next->next->next->next->next->prev = dll.head->next->next->next->next; dll.head->next->next->next->next->next->next = new Node(6); dll.head->next->next->next->next->next->next->prev = dll.head->next->next->next->next->next; dll.mergeSort(); return dll.head; } }; Solution solution; Node* result = solution.solve(); while (result) { cout << result->data << ' '; result = result->next; } return 0;
